rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order For Sanctions And To Compel Kaman Liu To Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2022 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANT TONY FU'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER FOR SANCTIONS AND TO COMPEL KAMAN LIU TO DEPOSITION is GRANTED. Deposition shall proceed in person at the Court reporter's office on February 23, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. Request for sanctions is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAMIR HINN ET AL VS. DIANA TOM SHARE TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Scott R Hodges, Successor Trustee Of The Diana Tom Share Trust For Judgment On The Pleadings As To The Second Amended Complaint'S First, Second And Fourth Causes Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT SCOTT HODGES' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT'S FIRST, SECOND AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION is DENIED. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges independent wrongful act and outrageous conduct by the defendant. Any factual disputes regarding Defendant's exercise of business judgment is not properly addressed on this motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAMIR HINN ET AL VS. DIANA TOM SHARE TRUST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT DIANA TOM'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Pursuant to May 30, 2015 order attached as Ex. A to Defendant's request for Judicial Notice, Ms. Tom stipulated/agreed that all issues relating to the management of the property are the domain of the trustee, including management of Ms. Tom's interest in the property; Ms. Tom is entitled to 50% of the net rental income. The lease was entered into after Hodges took over management of 100% of the property per stipulation and order. Any factual disputes regarding Tom's joining the lease or Tom's involvement or lack thereof in management of the property are issues of fact that cannot be properly addressed on a demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STEEL ARC PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. DS BROS. LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT DS BROS. LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action based on failure to pay rent that became due in full under the terms of the lease on the 1st of each time and for late fees due under the lease that became due within 5 days of rent due date. Defendant's argument that the Notice is not made in the disjunctive is not supported by the text of the notice; defendant's argument that the notice was not served in accordance with CCP 1161.3 is not supported by authority or any reference to the Complaint; defendant's brand new argument improperly included for the first time in the reply appears to assert that the Notice demanded rent for only 9 days of November, while the Notice states that the rent for the entire month of November became due on the first of the month and as of the date of the Notice (November 9, 2021) the rent for the entire month remained unpaid. If defendant disputes that full month rent for November 2021 was due as of November 9, 2021 (as opposed to daily payments up to November 9, 2021) or that the outstanding amount demanded was incorrect, such dispute cannot be presented on a demurrer, and especially not for the first time in the reply. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
